extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT Wout won stage 1 from a reduced bunch sprint after RICCITELLO Sergio's attack 2.9km out fragmented the group on the Cat 4 finishing climb.
tactical_patterns: RICCITELLO attacked 2.9km out on Cat 4 climb fragmenting group, revealing finishing gradients steep enough to drop pure sprinters.
ROGLIC positioned third wheel after RICCITELLO move, showing Jumbo-Visma's comfort letting van Aert finish rather than controlling earlier.
LATOUR's counter-attack with 2km remaining exploited altitude training advantage, suggesting high-altitude prep critical for Dauphiné climbs.
IMPEY's outside sprint closed 35kph to van Aert's 31kph in final 50m, nearly catching winner—positioning cost him victory.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Won from reduced bunch after Cat 4 climb, showing remarkable recovery one year post-Tour crash hospitalization.
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: Sat up on final climb after being sick, concerning form signal despite team claiming managed recovery effort.
rider_signals.IMPEY Daryl: positive: Nearly caught van Aert with superior closing speed but boxed in, demonstrating stronger finishing kick than positioning suggested.
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: Third wheel positioning after Riccitello attack but let van Aert finish, tactical deference or lacking final punch unclear.
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: Third place from bunch sprint despite coming from further back than Impey, solid climbing form evident.
rider_signals.VAN GARDEREN Tejay: negative: Dropped decisively on early climbs despite two previous runner-up finishes at this race, significant form decline.
rider_signals.BONIFAZIO Niccolo: negative: Abandoned breakaway with back pain despite leading KOM, forced out while in competitive position.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: Only 13th place on finish suited to his characteristics, unexpectedly weak on punchy Cat 4 finale.
